ops.title: How to read token/IndexedDB airline sessions and Amex MR — relaunch Arc with a CDP debug port and attach agent-browser; use tracked tab new/close
ops.body:
The way to read award balances that live in localStorage/IndexedDB (Delta SkyMiles, JetBlue TrueBlue) or behind Amex's device-risk step-up — none of which carry via `cookiesync` cookie replay — is to attach to the user's **real** Arc session:

1. Quit Arc gracefully so tab restore works: `osascript -e 'tell application "Arc" to quit'`.
2. Relaunch with a debug port: `open -a Arc --args --remote-debugging-port=9222`. Arc (Chromium 150) exposes the port even on the default profile, so the user's real logins/IndexedDB are live. Confirm with `curl http://127.0.0.1:9222/json/version`.
3. Drive it with `agent-browser --cdp 9222 …`.

Critical gotcha: `agent-browser --cdp … open <url>` HIJACKS the user's active tab (navigates it) and only tracks that one tab, so repeated reads drift onto whatever user tab it bound to (I kept reading a Hacker News tab). Instead use tracked tabs: `agent-browser --cdp 9222 tab new --label rd <url>`, snapshot the current tab, then `tab close rd` — this opens/closes its own tab and never touches the user's tabs. `curl .../json/list | jq` audits tab state read-only.

Results this session (2026-07, user yasyf): the real session cleared everything cookie-replay could not — Delta 370,619 (no Medallion, tier null), JetBlue 45,758 (no Mosaic, tier null), Amex Membership Rewards 547,933 (read off global.americanexpress.com/rewards/summary — the risk step-up does NOT fire on the genuine device). Still logged-OUT even in Arc: qatar, aa, singapore, capitalone, aeroplan (those logins weren't in this Arc profile). Downsides: the early `open` hijack closed two user tabs before I switched to `tab new`; leave Arc running and tell the user a normal manual restart drops the debug port and restores their session. Related: [[cookiesync-arc-dead-peer]].
